own_review.point: fetch() has no response.ok check or try-catch; worse, the URL points to master/disclaimer.txt which does not exist on master until this PR merges, so PR/preview builds render GitHub's literal '404: Not Found' body inside the <pre> (chicken-and-egg). Codebase has better patterns: Website/app/docs/layout.jsx wraps fetch in try-catch; Website/app/download/page.tsx shows error UI with Retry.

Fetching a file that lives in the same repository over the network from a mutable 'master' ref; privacy-policy/page.tsx pins a commit SHA. Better: read disclaimer.txt from the filesystem at build time, removing the raw.githubusercontent.com dependency entirely.
Disclaimer text is duplicated in README.md and disclaimer.txt; the two copies will drift over time — single source preferable.

Unexplained Footer layout changes beyond adding the link: flex-1 -> flex-auto changes the middle column from equal-basis to content-sized (breaks 3-column symmetry), and space-x-6 -> space-x-4 tightens spacing inconsistently with the other footer sections.
Unrelated package-lock.json churn (@eslint/plugin-kit 0.3.1->0.3.4 with nested @eslint/core 0.15.1) bundled into a docs/website PR.
PR description says content is 'loaded from my fork' but the committed URL points to the main repo; description is stale/contradictory and the preview deployment cannot show real content until merge.
No caching/revalidate strategy on the fetch; Next 15 defaults fetch to uncached, so caching intent (force-cache or revalidate) should be explicit.

points.why: Verified: both cited files do have the claimed error handling; real build-crash risk

No explicit caching/revalidate strategy on the fetch

qualitative: The human review is a content-free 'looks good to merge', so all three machine reviews vastly exceed the reference in substance. v4 is the best overall: it is the only one to catch both deeper structural issues — the mutable master ref versus the pinned-SHA convention in privacy-policy, and the README/disclaimer.txt content duplication — and it accurately verified codebase error-handling patterns, missing only the stale fork-URL note in the PR description and an explicit caching directive. v3 ties on net score with solid breadth (lockfile churn, fork discrepancy, revalidate) but only half-reports the Footer class change and slightly muddles Next.js caching semantics. v2 is the weakest: accurate but narrowest, missing the lockfile churn, the duplication, and the master-pinning issue. Notably, none of the three (nor the human) spotted the sharpest defect: disclaimer.txt does not exist on master until this PR merges, so PR-time and preview builds render GitHub's literal '404: Not Found' body as the page content — the no-error-handling points only gesture at this generically. No fabrications were found in any candidate; every codebase claim they made verified clean.
